name: Chili Verde
recipeIngredient: 1 can diced green chiles (6 oz.) – mid-heat range
recipeInstructions:
1. If fresh tomatillos are unavailable, substitute with prepared green taco sauces made from tomatillos. Avoid using too much hot sauce as it may overpower the chili.
2. Cut pork into bite-sized pieces and sauté in 1/4 cup peanut oil on low heat until browned.
3. In a processor, blend tomatillos, onion, garlic cloves, 1/4 cup oil, jalapeno peppers, and diced green chilies until smooth (remove seeds for milder chili).
4. Add chicken broth to the pork mixture and simmer over medium heat.
5. Gradually add the tomatillo/chili mixture to the broth and pork, stirring continuously. Simmer for about 1 hour at a low temperature, ensuring it just bubbles but does not boil.
6. Stir in the flour thickener until fully dissolved. Adjust consistency with more water or chicken broth if needed.
7. If the mixture has not thickened adequately, add cornstarch mixed with 1 teaspoon of water to achieve the desired thickness.
8. Fifteen minutes before serving, season with garlic granules, salt, black or white pepper, and cumin to taste.
Bon Appétit
